novel_features.name: Country value leaderboard
novel_features.description: Rank the best hotels across an entire country by Hotelist rating-per-dollar, with compound amenity and price filters.
novel_features.rationale: Hotelist's map UI is bounded to one city viewport; only a local mirror can compute a national value leaderboard from a single country-filtered pull.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs the single best-value hotel in a whole country under hard constraints, reach for this instead of paging city-by-city search.
novel_features.group: Cross-location value the map UI can't express

novel_features.name: Chain vs chain value showdown
novel_features.description: Compare hotel chains head-to-head on mean rating, median price, and rating-per-dollar in a country.
novel_features.rationale: The website filters one chain at a time with no aggregate stats; cross-chain comparison requires joining many per-chain pulls in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'which chain is actually worth it here?' in one call instead of a dozen manual map filters.
novel_features.group: Cross-location value the map UI can't express

novel_features.name: Nomad corridor scout
novel_features.description: Find the best hotel in each stop of a multi-city route in one pass, with shared filters.
novel_features.rationale: Requires resolving each city to its geohash via the local table and aggregating per-city winners across a user-defined corridor.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Plans a nomad's annual route in one command; pick this over N separate searches when the user names several cities.
novel_features.group: Cross-location value the map UI can't express

novel_features.name: Watch & drift tracker
novel_features.description: Snapshot a saved location over time and diff which hotels improved, declined, or changed price since you last checked.
novel_features.rationale: Hotelist has no historical state; only the local SQLite mirror stores timestamped scrape runs to diff against.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The only way to answer 'did this city's hotels get better or more expensive since last time?' — pick it for any change-over-time question.

novel_features.name: Chain consistency score
novel_features.description: Compute mean, median, and spread of a single chain's ratings across a country to see if the brand is reliably good or full of outliers.
novel_features.rationale: A population-level variance stat over all of a chain's properties is unavailable on the website; it is a pure aggregation on the local mirror.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before trusting a loyalty brand in an unfamiliar region; surfaces hidden outlier risk a single listing can't show.

novel_features.name: Price cliff finder
novel_features.description: Find the price point in a city where rating-per-extra-dollar collapses — the cheapest hotel that's still legitimately good.
novel_features.rationale: The site's price histogram is visual-only; extracting the breakpoint needs the full joint price+rating distribution stored locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns 'spend the least without sacrificing quality' into one number plus the hotels just below the cliff.

narrative.headline: Every Hotelist feature, plus a local SQLite mirror that ranks rating-per-dollar across whole countries, compares chains head-to-head, and tracks how a city's hotels change over time.
narrative.value_prop: Hotelist.com (by @levelsio) rates hotels with AI: it reads real traveler reviews, scores actual room photos, and photo-verifies claimed amenities so you can filter on a gym with real weights or a bathtub that actually exists. This CLI puts that data in your terminal and in agent context with --json/--select output, an offline cache, and cross-location commands the single-map website can't express: rank-country, chain-compare, corridor, and a watch/diff drift tracker. Data is scraped from Hotelist (community/AI-rated, not an official API).

quickstart.comment: Health check: confirms Hotelist.com is reachable before you search.
AI-rated hotels in a city, sorted by Hotelist Score.
Photo-verified amenities: a real weightlifting gym and a pool.
National value leaderboard the website's map UI can't produce.

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns an empty list for a city
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'hotelist-pp-cli sync cities' to refresh the city-to-geohash table, then retry; the city name must match a Hotelist city.
Hotelist prices are AI-estimated nightly figures, not live date-specific quotes; --checkin/--checkout are display context only, not a backend filter.
Add --fresh to bypass the local cache and re-fetch from Hotelist; default cache is fresh-on-read with a domain-appropriate stale window.
The CLI rate-limits politely by design; lower concurrency with --max-scan-pages on multi-location commands or wait and retry.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user wants AI-rated, photo-verified hotel discovery without pay-to-play bias, especially for cross-location questions: best value across a country, chain-vs-chain comparison, a multi-city route, or tracking how a place changes over time. It shines when the answer requires aggregating or joining hotel data that Hotelist's one-city-at-a-time map cannot.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to book a hotel or check live room availability — Hotelist is discovery-only with no booking or dated pricing.
Do not use it for date-specific nightly quotes; prices are AI-estimated averages.
Do not use it as a general Booking.com/Expedia client — it only exposes Hotelist's AI-rated dataset.

recipes.title: Best-value hotel with a real gym in a city
recipes.explanation: Photo-verified weightlifting gym, narrowed to just name/rating/price so an agent doesn't parse the full payload.

recipes.title: National value leaderboard
recipes.explanation: Top 10 hotels by rating-per-dollar across the whole country in one call.

recipes.title: Compare loyalty chains before committing
recipes.explanation: Head-to-head mean rating, median price, and value per chain.

recipes.title: Plan a nomad route
recipes.explanation: Best hotel per stop that clears all filters, in one pass.

recipes.title: Deep-dive one hotel's AI breakdown
recipes.explanation: Full Hotelist Score, AI photo and review ratings, consensus, verified amenities, pros and cons.

trigger_phrases: hotels with a real gym in
rank hotels by rating per dollar
